app.title: Email Export Import Tool
dash.heading: Migrations
dash.empty: No migrations yet — start one below.
dash.duplicate: A migration for this account pair already exists
resume.title: Enter passwords to resume
cancel.confirm_body: Progress so far is saved — you can resume later and it continues where it left off, without duplicates.
cert.title: Certificate cannot be verified
cert.body: The server presented a certificate that cannot be verified (often self-signed). The connection stays encrypted, but an attacker on the network could impersonate the server and capture your password. Continue only if you trust this server and network.
plan.title: Migration plan
progress.title: Migrating…
progress.cancelling: Stopping… current messages are being finished
done.title: Finished
done.failures_heading: Messages that could not be moved
done.resume_hint: You can run the app again anytime — it continues where it left off without duplicates.
done.quota: The destination mailbox is full. Free up space and run again to continue.
error.auth: The server rejected this email/password. Check the address and the app password.
error.connection: Could not connect to the server. Check the server name and your network.
plan.spool: Keep downloaded messages on disk until uploaded (failed uploads retry from disk without re-downloading)
done.spool_pending: {count} messages kept on disk for retry
settings.title: Settings
settings.data_note: Migration progress is saved here. Nothing is deleted — completed migrations stay listed as done.
plan.preserve_info: Folders, read/starred flags, dates and attachments are always preserved.
update.up_to_date: You are on the latest version.
update.failed: Update failed — please download it manually from the releases page.
update.ready: The installer has been opened. Close this app to finish updating.
sync.hint: Re-runs this migration and copies only messages that arrived since — nothing is duplicated.
bulk.title: Bulk migration
bulk.row_invalid: Row {n}: fill in the email and both passwords.
bulk.dest_invalid: Enter the destination server and a valid port.
settings.max_active_note: Bulk migrations run at most this many at once; the rest wait in a queue. Lower this if your server rejects too many logins.
settings.workers_note: How many messages a transfer moves at once. Lower is slower but gentler on the network and the server.
settings.rate_limit_note: Caps how fast messages are uploaded. Sustained full-speed transfer is what stresses a machine's network stack — lower this if transfers make your system unstable.
settings.safe_mode_note: One connection, one transfer at a time, 2 MB/s. Slowest but gentlest.
settings.tso_body: Your Mac hands oversized blocks to the network card to split up (TSO). Sustained bulk upload with this enabled has been seen crashing the macOS kernel. Turning it off costs a little speed and reverts on reboot. Run in Terminal:
